key_insight: QOC-Workbench uses an LLM as an autonomous quantum co-scientist to invent and validate cross-paradigm quantum optimal control protocols that go beyond parameter tuning.
engineering_relevance: The approach helps translate quantum control ideas into hardware-compliant protocols for different platforms, reducing expert design effort and improving practicality under strict experimental constraints.
method: LLM reads physics literature and proposes structural hypotheses for control protocols
Workflow writes and runs simulation code to test candidate controls
Builds cross-paradigm control motifs and, in one case, amortizes variational counterdiabatic driving with a GNN generator
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful demonstrations on three settings—Rydberg MIS arrays, XXZ spin chains, and random transverse-field Ising models—with discovered controls that outperform literature baselines and transfer to larger unseen systems.
watch_item: Because the workflow depends on LLM-generated hypotheses and simulation validation, robustness, reproducibility, and hardware transfer still need careful verification on real devices.
paper_title: LLM-Driven Cross-Paradigm Design for Quantum Optimal Control
